causal_chains.description: Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed
Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex
Excess apex speed causes understeer (high lateral load)

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 1 (11.1% – 16.7% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.159s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 750.2 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Late throttle application compromises corner exit speed (confidence: 67%)
    💡 Tip: Get on the throttle earlier and more decisively on corner exit to maximise straight-line speed.
    → Insufficient braking leads to excess speed at the apex (confidence: 29%)
    💡 Tip: Try initiating your braking earlier and with more initial pressure to scrub speed before the turn-in point.
zones.llm_feedback: You're currently running 1.2 km/h slower than the expert's speed through this segment, and the data suggests that late throttle application is a key factor, compromising your corner exit speed. To improve, focus on applying the throttle earlier, aiming to match the expert's RPM, which is currently 750.2 rpm higher than yours. Additionally, try to reduce your steering wheel angle by 1.0 ° to mitigate understeer, and consider adjusting your braking point to avoid excess speed at the apex. By making these adjustments, you should be able to carry more speed through the corner and improve your overall lap time.

By making these adjustments, you'll be able to carry more speed through the apex and improve your overall lap time.

causal_chains.description: Braking too early causes low apex speed
Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application
Aggressive throttle application causes rear instability

zones.template_feedback:
━━━ Zone 5 (54.1% – 57.7% of lap) ━━━
  Severity: ██████████ (100%)
  Estimated time impact: ~0.240s

  Key deviations:
    ▼ RPM: Engine RPM is lower than expected — check your gear selection
      Deviation: 734.6 rpm below expert level

  Root cause analysis:
    → Braking too early causes low apex speed (confidence: 100%)
    💡 Tip: You can brake later and carry more speed into the corner — trust the car's braking capability.
    → Low apex speed leads to premature throttle application (confidence: 70%)
    💡 Tip: Low mid-corner speed is tempting you to get on throttle early. Focus on carrying more apex speed instead.
zones.llm_feedback: You're carrying 11.4 km/h more speed than the expert through this segment, which is a great start. However, to optimize your lap, focus on braking later and lighter, as you're currently braking 2.7% more than the expert. Aim to reduce your braking intensity and delay your braking point to increase your apex speed, which will allow you to apply the throttle more smoothly and avoid premature acceleration. By doing so, you'll be able to match the expert's RPM, which is currently 734.6 rpm higher than yours.

summary_llm: The largest area for improvement lies in managing corner exit speed, with a total estimated time loss of ~0.549s. To address this, focus on applying the throttle earlier, particularly in Zone 1 where late throttle application results in being 750.2 rpm under optimal RPM. Despite these challenges, it's encouraging to see that the driver is consistently pushing the limits, as evidenced by the 100% severity rating across all zones. By making this adjustment, the driver can work towards reclaiming some of the lost time and improving their overall lap performance.
